political.s: <cite index="17-2,17-3">SiriusXM made $103,799 in political contributions in 2022 and spent $690,000 lobbying in 2021</cite>. The company has bipartisan political engagement through its diverse programming channels, though its parent company Liberty Media (prior to 2024 spinoff) has made substantial Republican donations through leadership.

charity.s: <cite index="11-4">SiriusXM Cares supports charitable organizations across three pillars: Access & Opportunity, Arts & Cultural Preservation, and Stronger Communities</cite>. <cite index="13-6">During 2024, over 590 employees volunteered over 8,400 hours, and over 680 employees utilized the charitable matching program, benefiting more than 860 charitable organizations</cite>.
charity.focus: Arts, cultural preservation, access & opportunity, community support

environment.s: <cite index="21-3,21-4">SiriusXM is measuring greenhouse gas emissions and is in the early stages of its sustainability journey, actively exploring opportunities to reduce GHG emissions through renewable energy sources</cite>. <cite index="21-14,21-15">The company has established a Supplier Code of Conduct requiring suppliers to comply with environmental laws and increase efficiency to reduce carbon footprint and energy use</cite>.

labor.s: <cite index="10-30,10-31">In March 2023, SiriusXM announced it would cut 475 employees (8% of its workforce), with CEO Jennifer Witz citing economic uncertainty and a need to operate with greater agility and efficiency</cite>. Employee reviews indicate mixed sentiment on morale and work-life balance.

dei.s: <cite index="47-2">SiriusXM's workforce includes 42% women and 40% people of color, with an executive team that includes 33% women and 17% people of color</cite>. <cite index="41-21,41-22">The Pathways Program places recent college graduates and early-career professionals from HBCUs into full-time roles with 12-month training, mentorship, and networking opportunities</cite>.

privacy.s: <cite index="51-1">SiriusXM uses device data for business purposes including delivering personalized services, understanding usage, preventing fraud, and for advertising identification across devices</cite>. <cite index="51-17,51-19">The company uses personal information to serve third-party advertising and some practices may be considered targeted advertising or sale/share of personal information under certain US state laws</cite>.

execPay.s: <cite index="67-1,67-2">SiriusXM CEO Jennifer Witz's total pay package was $37 million in 2024, comprised of a $2 million base salary with stock awards of $16.2 million and option awards of $16.5 million</cite>. <cite index="66-1,66-5">Her compensation surged 418%, though annual bonuses for top executives were reduced to 43% of target due to missing revenue and subscriber growth goals</cite>.
execPay.ratio: Not disclosed in standard CEO-to-worker ratio

fec.committeeName: SIRIUS XM RADIO INC. AND PANDORA MEDIA, LLC PAC (SIRIUSXM PANDORA PAC)

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
